context.story_engine: A high school teacher builds a drug empire, testing how far he'll go for family and control

plotlines.name: Walt: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive Gus's plan to replace and kill him
plotlines.obstacle: Gus's manipulation of Jesse, isolation from allies, lack of resources

plotlines.name: Jesse: Loyalty
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose within Gus's organization
plotlines.obstacle: guilt over killing Gale, Walt's manipulation, conflicting allegiances

plotlines.name: Gus: Vengeance
plotlines.goal: destroy the cartel and complete his decades-long revenge
plotlines.obstacle: cartel's power, Walt's unpredictability, DEA investigation

plotlines.name: Hank: Investigation
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth behind Gale's murder and the blue meth operation
plotlines.obstacle: physical disability, DEA bureaucracy, lack of direct evidence
plotlines.stakes: career vindication, public safety, family danger

plotlines.name: Skyler: Laundering
plotlines.goal: successfully launder Walt's drug money through the car wash
plotlines.obstacle: overwhelming cash volume, maintaining legitimate appearance, Ted's complications
plotlines.stakes: IRS exposure, family destruction, criminal prosecution

events.event: Gus arrives at the superlab where Walt and Jesse are held at gunpoint by Mike
Gus changes into work clothes without speaking to Walt or Jesse
Gus slashes Victor's throat with a box cutter in front of Walt, Jesse, and Mike
Gus tells Walt and Jesse to get back to work after cleaning himself
Walt and Jesse dissolve Victor's body in hydrofluoric acid and clean the lab in stunned silence
Skyler drives to Walt's condo and finds the door locked
Skyler talks her way past the landlord claiming Walt may be injured inside
Skyler finds the condo empty and leaves repeated voicemails Walt cannot answer
Walt comes home visibly shaken but tells Skyler nothing about what happened
Skyler presses forward with the car wash plan despite Walt's state, reminding him about Bogdan's refusal
Hank works obsessively from bed on the Gale Boetticher murder case
Hank examines Gale's notebook and finds chemistry notes suggesting a large-scale meth operation
Hank tells Gomez that Gale may have been the cook behind the blue meth
Jesse sits alone at Denny's restaurant staring blankly, deeply traumatized
Walt visits Jesse and finds him in a fragile state
Walt urges Jesse to hold himself together because Gus is looking for reasons to eliminate them
Walt buys a snub-nosed revolver from a black-market dealer

interactions.description: all three storylines explore what characters sacrifice to survive - Walt and Jesse their humanity, Skyler her moral boundaries
Hank's theory that Gale was the mastermind actually protects Walt, while the audience knows Walt is the real target
Skyler doesn't know Walt nearly died and is planning armed resistance while she pushes forward with money laundering

events.event: Walt purchases a .38 revolver and practices drawing it, rehearsing shooting Gus
Walt goes to Los Pollos Hermanos asking to see Gus
Mike intercepts Walt in the parking lot and takes his gun
Mike tells Walt that Gus will never put himself in a vulnerable position
Walt realizes direct confrontation is impossible and needs a different strategy
Jesse hosts a continuous party with drug users to avoid being alone with his thoughts
Jesse uses his meth earnings to keep the party running around the clock
Jesse sits in the chaos looking hollow and disconnected
When the house empties, Jesse immediately turns music back up and invites more people
Walt visits Jesse to discuss strategy but sees he's in no condition to plan
Hank redirects his frustration into investigating Gale's lab notes
Marie patiently manages Hank's care while he treats her dismissively
Hank asks Gomez to bring more evidence files from the Boetticher case
Gomez reluctantly complies, noting the case is officially winding down
Skyler hires an environmental inspector to flag the car wash for contamination
Skyler presents the contamination scheme to Walt as pressure to force Bogdan to sell
Walt is impressed by Skyler's resourcefulness but unsettled by her natural deception
Mike meets with Gus to discuss the Walt problem
Gus tells Mike to be patient, signaling he has a longer-term plan
Mike expresses concern that Walt and Jesse's instability increases exposure risk

interactions.description: all storylines explore characters trying to control their circumstances through increasingly desperate or deceptive means
the audience knows Gus and Mike are planning Walt's demise while Walt seeks strategy, and that Hank is investigating the very operation Walt runs
Walt's need for Jesse as an ally conflicts with Jesse's psychological breakdown after killing Gale

events.event: Marie visits open houses under false identities and steals small decorative items
Marie adopts elaborate fictional personas and tells invented stories to real estate agents
Marie attends multiple open houses in a single day, stealing with increasing boldness
A real estate agent grows suspicious and confronts Marie, who deflects and leaves quickly
Skyler's environmental pressure tactic forces Bogdan to face potential regulatory scrutiny
Skyler negotiates aggressively with Bogdan for the car wash sale
Bogdan agrees to sell the A1A Car Wash below his original asking price
Walt and Skyler officially own the car wash as their money laundering front
Skyler begins studying how to run the legitimate car wash operation
Jesse's house party continues with him handing out cash freely to anyone
A partygoer steals money from Jesse's drawer while Jesse doesn't notice or care
Walt visits Jesse and grows alarmed at his deteriorating self-destructive behavior
Jesse refuses to engage with Walt about Gus or the lab work
Walt cooks alone at the superlab because Jesse shows up late and impaired
Walt tells Jesse their survival depends on flawless performance since Gus seeks any excuse to replace them
Jesse responds with indifference, saying Gus will kill them eventually regardless
Hank finds a handwritten dedication to 'W.W.' in Gale's notebook
Hank puzzles over the W.W. initials but doesn't connect them to Walt
Hank casually mentions the W.W. notation to Marie as an interesting lead

episodes.theme: the illusion of control through self-destruction

interactions.description: all three storylines show characters seeking control through destructive means — Marie through theft, Jesse through self-destruction, Skyler through criminal enterprise
the audience knows Walt is W.W. and that Hank's discovery threatens Walt's survival, but Hank doesn't realize the connection
Jesse's self-destructive spiral directly threatens Walt's survival strategy with Gus

events.event: Hank discovers Gale's karaoke video singing 'Major Tom'
Hank and Marie watch the video and find Gale endearing but telling
Hank invites Walt and Skyler for dinner to discuss the case
Skyler rehearses the gambling cover story with Walt
Skyler corrects Walt on details to keep their story airtight
Hank shows Walt Gale's lab notebook seeking his expert opinion
Walt downplays Gale's expertise, calling him a competent student not a genius
Walt tells Hank the real mastermind is probably still out there
Walt delivers the gambling cover story convincingly at dinner
Hank and Marie accept the gambling explanation without skepticism
Walt's deflection reignites Hank's curiosity about the case
Jesse's house party winds down with guests stripping everything valuable
Jesse sits alone in his empty, trashed living room
Mike arrives and orders Jesse to ride with him for pickups
Mike drives Jesse to various distribution points while Jesse sits doing nothing
Jesse spots a man approaching with apparent hostile intent but it's just a routine delivery
Walt grows suspicious when Jesse mentions spending the day with Mike
Walt confronts Jesse, warning him that Gus is manipulating him to replace Walt
Jesse dismisses Walt's concerns and calls him paranoid

interactions.description: all three storylines involve characters trying to control narratives and information, but their actions create unexpected complications
Walt's attempt to deflect suspicion from Gale actually reignites Hank's investigation, while Hank unknowingly seeks advice from the very criminal he's hunting
Walt correctly identifies Gus's manipulation of Jesse, but Jesse can't see it and views Walt as paranoid, not protective

events.event: Mike takes Jesse on money pickups across desert and rural New Mexico
A man attacks Mike and Jesse, trying to steal the money bag
Jesse grabs the bag and drives away in Mike's car, saving the cash
Mike dispatches the attacker and tells Jesse he handled himself well
Jesse shows genuine engagement and energy for the first time since killing Gale
Gus observes from a distance, confirming the entire scenario was staged
Mike acknowledges to Gus that the plan is working to build Jesse's loyalty
Walt works alone in the superlab, unable to maintain production schedule
Walt suspects Gus is deliberately separating him from Jesse to weaken his position
Walt confronts Jesse, insisting the ride-alongs are manipulation to make him compliant
Jesse pushes back, saying someone finally treats him with respect instead of belittling him
The argument exposes the fracture: Walt sees Jesse as subordinate, Jesse sees Walt as controlling
Skyler officially takes over A1A Car Wash operations, renaming it simply Car Wash
Skyler studies the books and establishes systems for funneling Walt's drug money
Skyler discovers Walt's cash volume far exceeds what the car wash can plausibly launder
Skyler begins looking for ways to increase apparent revenue without drawing IRS attention
Hank takes his first steps with a walker in physical therapy
Hank channels his frustration into the Boetticher case
Hank asks Gomez to check industrial equipment purchases matching Gale's superlab notes
Gomez pushes back saying the case isn't priority but agrees to run queries

interactions.description: all three storylines explore different forms of manipulation — Gus manipulating Jesse's loyalty, Walt trying to manipulate Jesse's perception, Skyler manipulating financial records
the audience knows Gus staged the attack to build Jesse's loyalty, while Walt correctly suspects manipulation but Jesse doesn't believe him
Hank's investigation gets closer to Walt's operation while Walt and Skyler remain unaware of the threat

events.event: Walt tells Skyler someone at work is trying to kill him
Skyler suggests they use their money to disappear
Walt refuses to run, insisting he will not lose control
Skyler sends Holly and Walter Jr. to stay with Hank and Marie
Walt tries to recruit laundry workers to spy on Gus
Laundry workers refuse and report Walt to Tyrus
Tyrus informs Gus of Walt's desperate recruitment attempt
Mike assigns Jesse to collect payment from a skimming dealer
Jesse successfully handles the confrontation and collects the money
Jesse earns Mike's quiet approval and develops working rapport
Hank visits the DEA office using a walking cane
Hank examines Gale's evidence and finds Los Pollos Hermanos napkin
Hank takes the evidence list home to study further
Skyler processes large batch of cash at the car wash
Skyler realizes cash is accumulating faster than business can absorb

interactions.description: all three storylines explore characters finding their place and purpose under pressure - Walt desperately trying to maintain control, Jesse finding his role in Gus's organization, Skyler taking control of what she can
the audience knows Walt is Heisenberg and that Hank is getting closer to the truth through Gale's evidence, while neither character knows about the other's activities
Hank's physical recovery enables his return to investigative work, merging his personal and professional storylines

events.event: Jesse attends Narcotics Anonymous meeting and tells story of killing 'problem dog'
Jesse admits to the group he's really talking about killing a person
Jesse storms out when group won't judge him for the killing
Walt asks Jesse to help kill Gus using ricin poison
Walt argues Gus will kill them both once he finds replacement cook
Jesse agrees to carry ricin capsule but doesn't commit to using it
Jesse conceals ricin capsule inside marked cigarette
Hank presents theory that Gale was murdered as part of larger drug operation
Hank shows Walt Los Pollos Hermanos connection from Gale's effects
Walt deflects Hank's theory while privately recognizing the danger
Mike reports to Gus that Jesse is becoming reliable and loyal
Gus approves bringing Jesse further in to make Walt expendable
Mike warns Gus that Walt is unpredictable and may act desperately
Gus increases surveillance on Walt through Tyrus
Skyler tells Walt they need to discuss exit strategy
Walt refuses to elaborate on how he'll resolve Gus situation

interactions.description: all three storylines explore characters grappling with the moral weight of their choices - Jesse seeking judgment for killing Gale, Walt justifying murder as survival, Gus calculating human expendability
Hank unknowingly shares his investigation progress with the very person he's hunting
Walt's ricin plan forces Jesse to choose between his growing loyalty to Gus and his partnership with Walt, while Gus moves to eliminate Walt

events.event: Young Gus and Max meet with Don Eladio at his poolside estate to pitch a meth partnership
Max explains the chemistry and profitability of meth over cocaine to Don Eladio
Don Eladio takes offense at Gus and Max's presumptuousness in distributing samples without permission
Hector mocks Gus's Chilean background and shows contempt for him
Hector shoots and kills Max at the poolside on Don Eladio's orders
Gus is forced to watch Max's execution and lies next to his body at the pool's edge
Gus visits wheelchair-bound Hector at Casa Tranquila nursing home
Gus informs Hector that the Salamanca family influence is finished and his nephews are dead
Hank requests a DEA background check on Gustavo Fring
The background check on Gus comes back clean with no criminal record
ASAC Merkert warns Hank that Gus is a DEA friend and donor, telling him to drop the investigation
Hank agrees to back off officially but continues examining Gale's notes at home
Walt grows anxious when Jesse reports he hasn't found an opportunity to use the ricin on Gus
Walt presses Jesse to act quickly with the ricin, but Jesse insists he needs the right moment

interactions.description: all storylines deal with past actions driving present consequences — Gus's trauma motivates his revenge, Hank's investigation uncovers hidden history, Walt's past choices trap him in survival mode
the audience knows Gus's true nature and history while Hank sees only a clean businessman, and Walt doesn't understand the depth of Gus's long-term planning
Walt's desperation to survive increasingly conflicts with Jesse's growing loyalty to Gus's organization

events.event: Walt plants a GPS tracking device on Gus's car
Walt monitors Gus's routine from his laptop and shares intel with Jesse
Walt urges Jesse to use the ricin at the first opportunity
Jesse remains noncommittal about using the ricin
Gus invites Jesse to dinner at his home and cooks Chilean fish stew
Gus tells Jesse he sees potential in him and that others have underestimated him
Jesse has the ricin cigarette but chooses not to use it during dinner
Jesse leaves Gus's house without telling Walt about the dinner
Hank discovers Gus's background prior to the 1980s is completely unverifiable
Hank shares his findings with Gomez, who warns him to leave Gus alone
Hank ignores the warning and requests additional records searches
Walt discovers GPS data shows Gus was home when Jesse was unaccounted for
Walt confronts Jesse and demands to know about his private meeting with Gus
Jesse admits to the dinner and Walt accuses him of switching sides
Jesse tells Walt that Gus treats him like a human being unlike Walt
Walt and Jesse fight physically in Jesse's living room until both are bloodied
Jesse tells Walt to leave and not come back
Walt discovers an identical tracking device on his own car
Walt realizes Gus knows everything about his surveillance

interactions.description: all three storylines explore different forms of betrayal and shifting allegiances — Walt's paranoia about Jesse's loyalty, Jesse's growing allegiance to Gus over Walt, and Hank's investigation threatening to expose hidden loyalties
the audience knows about Jesse's dinner with Gus before Walt does, and knows Walt is being tracked while he thinks he's the one doing the tracking
the storylines merge in the physical fight between Walt and Jesse, representing the complete breakdown of their partnership

events.event: Gus travels to Mexico with Jesse and Mike to meet the cartel
Jesse realizes he is the cook Gus is delivering to the cartel
Gus coaches Jesse on what to expect and tells him to be confident
Jesse takes command of the cartel lab, berating chemists for contaminated equipment
Jesse demands the lab be cleaned to his standards before cooking
Jesse produces a batch testing at 96.2 percent purity, impressing Don Eladio
Don Eladio tells Gus that Jesse will stay in Mexico permanently
Gus presents Don Eladio with poisoned Zafiro Añejo tequila at the poolside
Gus drinks first to prove the tequila is safe, having taken precautions
Don Eladio and his capos collapse and die from the poisoned tequila
Gus declares to Hector's allies that the cartel is finished
Mike is shot during the escape from the compound
Jesse drives while Mike bleeds and Gus is weakened by poison
Jesse keeps both men alive during the chaotic drive to medical team
Walt cooks alone in the superlab, isolated and increasingly desperate

interactions.description: both storylines involve proving worth through decisive action — Gus through his ultimate revenge, Jesse through his competence under pressure
Walt has no idea his fate is being decided in Mexico while he cooks alone
Jesse's demonstration of loyalty enables Gus's vengeance plan to succeed

events.event: Walt learns from news that Mexican cartel members are dead and suspects Gus was involved
Gus returns to Albuquerque recovering from poisoning and resumes operations
Walt asks Jesse about Mexico trip but Jesse provides minimal information
Hank cross-references Gale's lab notes with chemical suppliers and identifies Madrigal connection to Los Pollos Hermanos
Hank asks Walt to drive him to Los Pollos Hermanos for lunch
Hank asks Walt to plant GPS tracker on Gus's car while he waits in vehicle
Walt reluctantly attaches the GPS device to Gus's car
Walt warns Gus through phone call to check his car
Gus summons Walt to desert where Tyrus blindfolds and drives him
Gus fires Walt and warns he'll kill Hank if Walt interferes with Jesse or contacts DEA
Walt begs Gus to reconsider, arguing Hank is family investigating naturally
Walt tells Skyler they need to disappear immediately
Walt demands Saul contact identity provider, learns cost is $500,000 for family
Walt discovers crawl space nearly empty because Skyler gave money to Ted for IRS debt
Walt lies in crawl space laughing hysterically then screaming as Marie calls about Hank being in danger

interactions.description: all storylines involve characters trying to protect something — Walt protecting his life, Hank protecting justice, Skyler protecting their secret — but their protective actions create new dangers
Walt is forced to help Hank investigate the very criminal he's warning, creating a deadly triangle of deception
Skyler's money laundering protection directly undermines Walt's survival plan when he discovers the missing cash

events.event: Walt instructs Huell to steal Jesse's ricin cigarette and replace it with a regular one
Walt poisons Brock with Lily of the Valley berries from his backyard
Brock falls seriously ill and is hospitalized with ricin-like symptoms
Jesse rushes to the hospital when Andrea calls about Brock's condition
Jesse discovers the ricin cigarette is missing from his pack
Jesse concludes Gus poisoned Brock and goes to Walt's house with a gun
Walt convinces Jesse that Gus orchestrated the poisoning to turn Jesse against Walt
Jesse agrees to help Walt kill Gus
Hank traces GPS data from Gus's car to Lavanderia Brillante laundry
Hank tells Gomez the laundry may be the superlab site
DEA begins preliminary surveillance of the laundry
Skyler learns Ted was injured trying to flee instead of paying the IRS
Skyler sits silently in the car wash, horrified by the consequences of her actions
Jesse reveals Gus visits Hector at Casa Tranquila nursing home
Walt realizes Hector's hatred of Gus presents an opportunity

interactions.description: all three storylines involve characters crossing moral lines to protect what they value — Walt poisons a child, Jesse is manipulated into betrayal, Skyler's money laundering leads to violence
the audience knows Walt poisoned Brock and manipulated Jesse, while Jesse believes Gus is responsible
Walt's plan to use Hector against Gus connects Walt's survival with Gus's vulnerability to his old enemy

events.event: Walt visits Hector at Casa Tranquila and proposes an alliance against Gus
Walt tells Hector that Gus killed Don Eladio and destroyed the cartel
Hector agrees to Walt's plan by ringing his bell
Walt rigs a pipe bomb to Hector's wheelchair bell
Hector requests a DEA meeting through nursing home staff
Hank and Gomez visit Hector but he gives them nothing
Hector spells out 'SUCK MY' and rings his bell in defiance
Tyrus sweeps Hector's room but doesn't check the wheelchair
Gus enters and confronts Hector about what he told the DEA
Hector rings his bell and the bomb detonates, killing Hector, Tyrus, and Gus
Gus walks out with half his face blown away, then collapses dead
Jesse learns of Gus's death while working at the superlab
Jesse and Walt destroy the superlab with chemicals and fire
Walt calls Skyler and tells her 'I won'
Hospital confirms Brock was poisoned by Lily of the Valley, not ricin
Jesse accepts it was accidental and doesn't suspect Walt
Camera reveals Lily of the Valley plant in Walt's backyard
Hank's investigation is validated by the explosion and discovery of Gus's empire

interactions.description: all three storylines explore how far characters will go to achieve their goals and what they sacrifice in the process
the audience knows Walt poisoned Brock to manipulate Jesse, but Jesse doesn't
Walt's survival plan and Gus's vengeance storyline literally converge in the explosion that kills both Hector and Gus
the final reveal of the Lily of the Valley plant reframes Walt's entire season arc
